column1: A Tropical Storm WATCH has been issued from Pasco/Hernando Co line south (Aripeka) south. A Tropical Storm WARNING is in effect from Bonita Beach south. The WATCH means tropical storm conditions are possible within the next 48 hours.

Why is NOAA using the term cyclone now for the Gulf/Atlantic? In all my years in Florida (almost 30) I've only seen tropical wave/depression/storm on the NOAA sites for the Gulf/Atlantic and the term cyclone only used in the Pacific (I realize they are all cyclones, I'm just asking about the terminology being used). Have I just been missing it or has there been a change this year?
cyclone is the parent name. Hurricane and tropical storm name only comes into play once it reaches those characteristics
Hurricanes, tropical storms, typhoons are all tropical cyclones. The only difference is where in the world they form.

Facts 🛑
Q: What are Potential Tropical Cyclone Advisories?
A: These are issued before a tropical depression or tropical storm forms.
Q: What purpose do these advisories serve?
A: They provide the NWS with the ability to issue a watch with a full 48 hours of lead time or a warning with a full 36 hours of lead time prior to possible tropical storm, hurricane, or life-threatening storm surge conditions on land.
Q: What information should we expect?
ALL of us should see information containing all of the standard NHC advisory text and graphical products and should be issued at the standard advisory times, according to the Federal Government, NOAA/NWS.
